name: rust-cli-app
description: A command-line application built in Rust with Clap for argument parsing, Tokio for async I/O, and thiserror/anyhow for structured error handling. Includes a library crate alongside the binary for testability, with unit and integration tests using the standard Rust test harness.
unified_instruction:
# Rust CLI Application

## Stack
- **Language**: Rust (2021 edition)
- **Args**: Clap v4 (derive API)
- **Async**: Tokio (if I/O-bound)
- **Errors**: thiserror (library), anyhow (binary)
- **Logging**: tracing + tracing-subscriber
- **Config**: config crate or environment variables

## Crate Structure
```
src/
  main.rs          # Entry point — thin, parses args, calls lib
  lib.rs           # Public API and business logic
  cli.rs           # Clap struct definitions
  error.rs         # thiserror error types
tests/
  integration/     # Integration tests (black-box via CLI)
```

## Conventions
- `main.rs` is thin — all logic lives in `lib.rs` or submodules
- Use `thiserror` for typed library errors; `anyhow` in `main` for context wrapping
- Never use `unwrap()` in library code — propagate with `?`
- Add `#[cfg(test)]` unit tests alongside every non-trivial function
- Run `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` as part of CI; fix all lints

## Key Commands
```bash
cargo build --release       # Build optimised binary
cargo run -- <args>         # Run in dev mode
cargo test                  # Run all tests
cargo clippy -- -D warnings # Lint with all warnings as errors
cargo doc --open            # Browse generated docs
```
